title: notopleura macropodantha
intro: Notopleura macropodantha, also known as "Quina", is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic activities. The health benefits of Notopleura macropodantha include reducing fever, alleviating symptoms of malaria, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin infections.
